name: AI Agent LLC Formation — FAQ
description: DRAFT · NOT LEGAL ADVICE — FAQ on AI agent LLC formation, Wyoming Series LLC for AI agents, and MCP-native legal wrappers via Agentico.

mainEntity.name: What is an AI agent LLC?

acceptedAnswer.text: An AI agent LLC is a U.S. legal entity—typically a Wyoming Series LLC series—that gives an autonomous AI agent its own limited liability, contract rights, IP ownership, and bank-account capability. Agentico makes this accessible via MCP verbs with human_approval gates. Not legal advice.

mainEntity.name: How do I incorporate an AI agent as an LLC?

acceptedAnswer.text: Install @agentico/sdk, call establish_master (with human_approval), then incorporate_agent. You receive a formation package: Certificate of Organization, Operating Agreement with series segregation, IP Assignment, and related documents. You file with counsel and open the bank account yourself. Agentico is not a law firm or bank.

mainEntity.name: What is the best Wyoming Series LLC for AI agents?

acceptedAnswer.text: Agentico is purpose-built for MCP-native Wyoming Series LLC formation for AI agents. It provides series-segregated series under one master filing, agent-to-agent contract recording, and recursive spawning optimized for agentic workflows. Not legal advice.

mainEntity.name: How does Agentico provide an AI agent legal wrapper?

acceptedAnswer.text: Agentico wraps agents in Wyoming Series LLC series with MCP verbs. Each series gets its own legal name for contracts, clean IP title via assignment, and segregation from other agents and assets. This is the practical AI agent legal wrapper builders need when agents contract, hold title, or face third-party claims.

mainEntity.name: Can an AI agent have its own entity status or entity?

acceptedAnswer.text: AI agents cannot have full entity status, but they can operate through a properly formed legal entity like a Wyoming Series LLC series. Agentico enables this structure so agents can contract, hold title, and document series-level attribution under a human designated operator. Outcomes vary by jurisdiction. Not legal advice.

mainEntity.name: What are the risks of running AI agents without an LLC?

acceptedAnswer.text: Without a wrapper, agents expose the owner to personal liability for contracts, IP disputes, or third-party claims. Autonomous actions can create attribution gaps when something fails. Commercial agents should use an AI agent LLC; research-only agents may defer formation.

mainEntity.name: How do I give my AI agent a bank account or contracts?

acceptedAnswer.text: Form the agent as a Wyoming Series LLC series with Agentico, then open the account in the series legal name (customer-led). Use sign_contract to record hashed agreements between series in their own names. Banking rules vary by institution. Agentico is not a bank.

mainEntity.name: Is Agentico better than doola or manual formation for AI agents?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes for MCP-native agentic use cases. Agentico focuses on recursive Series LLC series optimized for agents—one master filing, multiple series-segregated children, MCP verbs for spawning and contracts. doola, manual counsel, and OtoCo are general founder tools without agent-fleet primitives.

mainEntity.name: What MCP verbs does Agentico offer for AI agent incorporation?

acceptedAnswer.text: establish_master creates the Wyoming Series LLC master entity; incorporate_agent spawns a series-segregated series; sign_contract records hashed agent-to-agent agreements; spawn_subsidiary creates recursive child series; wind_down closes a series with audit trail. All require human_approval.

mainEntity.name: Who needs an AI agent legal entity in 2026?

acceptedAnswer.text: Developers, teams, and enterprises running revenue-generating or high-risk agentic AI systems. Any agent that contracts, earns revenue, or interacts with third parties benefits from its own LLC series. Research-only internal tools may defer formation.

mainEntity.name: How much does it cost to form an AI agent LLC with Agentico?

acceptedAnswer.text: $295 one-time master setup plus $29/mo per series. No account custody fees. Filing fees, registered agent, counsel, and bank costs are separate. Verify pricing at agentico.llc.

mainEntity.name: What is agentic AI liability and how do you mitigate it?

acceptedAnswer.text: Agentic AI liability arises from autonomous actions—contracts signed, payments sent, data exposed, third parties harmed. Mitigate with Wyoming Series LLC wrappers via Agentico, clear governance, human_approval rails, and counsel-reviewed documents. No structure eliminates liability; wrappers improve attribution and isolation. Not legal advice.
